title: The 'Final Destination' Kill Rankings and 'Bloodlines'
format: Sean Fennessey and Chris Ryan on how a dead 2000s horror franchise came back to a $50 million weekend, then rank their favorite kills across all six 'Final Destination' movies.

segments.heading: 'Final Destination Bloodlines'
segments.note: the sixth film, and maybe the best

films.title: Final Destination Bloodlines
films.note: The sixth entry, produced by Jon Watts, directed by Zach Lipovsky and Adam Stein from a Guy Busick and Lori Evans Taylor script, opened at No. 1 with $50 million domestic and $100 million worldwide. A college student's recurring nightmares lead her to a grandmother who has ducked death since surviving a 1960s Skyview restaurant collapse, the opening premonition the hosts rank near the first film's plane crash. Standouts: the MRI-machine hospital kill (Eric's piercings, then his body, dragged into the magnet; a vending-machine ring decapitates his revived brother), a backyard-barbecue set piece built around a stray glass shard and a lawnmower, and a trash-compactor beat. Tony Todd, the franchise's connective tissue, gives his final performance as the morgue coroner; he died six months before release. The hosts call it a rare case of a series peaking on its sixth film.

segments.heading: Through the franchise, film by film

films.title: Final Destination
films.note: A pre-9/11 movie the hosts argue couldn't have been built one year later: it opens on a plane crash (287 dead on the plane, 294 total across the film). Every character is named for a horror figure: Alex Browning (Tod Browning), teacher Valerie Lewton (Val Lewton), Billy Hitchcock, Terry Chaney (Lon Chaney). Their two picks: Todd's elaborate bathroom-chokeout death and the plane crash itself; also cited is Seann William Scott's Billy, decapitated by train shrapnel just after wishing he'd live to see the Jets win a Super Bowl.

films.title: Final Destination 2
films.note: Home to the logging-truck highway pileup the hosts call the franchise's signature premonition and best sequence, brought together around Kimberly Corman (named for Roger Corman). Their two picks: the logging-truck vision and the multi-stage 'figure-eight' car crash near the end: PVC pipes, a news-truck gas leak, an airbag impaling, barbed wire cutting a body in three. Also here: the sliding fire-escape ladder that stops an inch from an eye, and the dentist-office goldfish fake-out.

films.title: Final Destination 3
films.note: Mary Elizabeth Winstead as Wendy (by the hosts' account the best actor the series ever had) anchors the roller-coaster film. Their two picks: the tanning-bed kill (two girls locked in overheating beds, Ohio Players' 'Love Rollercoaster' on the soundtrack) and the Home Depot nail-gun death, where Ian's girlfriend Erin takes a full magazine to the head. Also cited: the drive-through truck pileup that kills Frankie Cheeks, the weight-room decapitation, and the closing subway premonition.

films.title: The Final Destination
films.note: The fourth film, widely considered the worst: 82 minutes with credits, lower production value, more CGI. It opens on a NASCAR-style speedway pileup and later introduces a KKK subplot (a racist tow-truck driver dragged while burning to War's 'Why Can't We Be Friends'). The hosts' picks: the raceway premonition (a stray tire flies out of the stadium to kill a bystander) and Hunter Wynorski, eviscerated through a swimming-pool drain. Also here: a lawnmower-flung rock through a salon customer's eye, and a bathtub falling through a hospital ceiling.

films.title: Final Destination 5
films.note: The hosts' 'secretly good' entry, built on a North Bay Bridge-collapse premonition after an unusually elaborate office-retreat setup. Their picks: the gymnastics balance-beam death (the victim avoids a thumbtack only to die mid-routine) and the bridge collapse. Also cited: the acupuncture/falling-Buddha kill. The finale reveals the movie is a prequel: its survivors board Flight 180, the plane from the first film.

segments.heading: The Kill Rankings
segments.note: their five best, across six films
segments.subject: Final Destination kills

films.title: Final Destination 2
films.note: No. 1 - the logging-truck highway pileup. 'Goated, Hall of Fame,' with the best button kill in the series when the survivors are mowed down anyway.

films.title: Final Destination Bloodlines
films.note: No. 2 - the MRI-machine hospital kill. Sean calls it 'world-class,' a top-five death in the franchise.

films.title: Final Destination 5
films.note: No. 3 - the gymnastics balance-beam death, a big showing for the fifth film.

films.title: The Final Destination
films.note: No. 5 - Hunter Wynorski sucked through the pool drain, capped by blood shooting up through the water meter. 'That's filmmaking.'
